comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PLAN REVIEW · CONTAINER · [G3] Child coverage — pairing (parent 6f2d ⟶ child b4af WS-EVAL-EXISTING) — VERDICT: CLEAN (no findings).

Scope note: the parent epic's AUTHORITATIVE residual scope (per the latest SCOPE-DECISION comment) narrowed to WS-DOC + WS-EVAL-EXISTING (WS-VISUAL-EVAL split off into its own epic; WS-MIGRATE/WS-PROMPTS/WS-CLEANUP/WS-VISUAL/WS-VALIDATE/WS-INTENT-ALIGN all marked DONE via closed sibling epics 5766/72d5/e6d9/681b). The 2-child roster is WS-DOC (4024) + WS-EVAL-EXISTING (b4af). This pairing reviews b4af against the WS-EVAL-EXISTING lane.

4-bucket audit of the parent's WS-EVAL-EXISTING acceptance/success criteria vs this child:
- Eval coverage for verify_completion / review_ticket(ticket-quality) / scan_spec(spec-alignment) — FULLY (Phase-1 authors the 3 specs; Phase-2 finishes the shared run machinery so they actually gate).
- Eval coverage for review_code (code-quality) — FULLY (covered-by-definition / ANTI-FP): the parent body names FOUR migrated reviewers (verify_completion + review_ticket + review_code + scan_spec), but the child's title/Phase-1 names only THREE. The fourth (review_code) is NOT absent — it is the pre-existing code-quality.eval.yaml, which the child explicitly brings under the gate via "all 7 specs" in AC2 (scorer registry maps every name used by all 7), AC3 (run_eval() live for all specs), and AC4 (eval-discipline gates all 7 on every PR). 7 = 4 existing (code-quality + plan-review finder/verifier/isf) + 3 new. Reconciles cleanly; not an uncovered-criterion finding.
- recall / false-accept / false-fire metrics — FULLY (structural): carried by AC1 non-empty gold_set + AC4 offline "dataset balance" assertion + Phase-2.4 fixture corpus & label adjudication, matching the established convention in the existing plan-review eval specs and parity.py.
- Drawn from the three corpuses (DSO read-only + snap-oakhart-manual + rebar tickets) — FULLY (Phase-2.4 fixture corpus + per-reviewer dataset schema; provenance is a property of that deliverable).
- "parallel-run-and-diff old vs new, made into a standing eval" — FULLY (the child's stated purpose: regression-gated, "not a blind cutover").
- Phase 1 + Phase 2 ("make evals actually run" — run_eval() stub, inert scorer names, placeholder eval-live step) — FULLY (Phase-2.1–2.5).

No parent criterion in this child's lane is left uncovered; the lone apparent gap (review_code) survives the roster/codebase cross-check as covered-by-definition. Empty findings list for G3 on this pairing.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PLAN REVIEW · CONTAINER · [G4] Child consistency — pairing (parent 6f2d ⟶ child b4af WS-EVAL-EXISTING), cross-checked against the complete authoritative 2-child roster [WS-DOC (4024) + WS-EVAL-EXISTING (b4af)] — VERDICT: CLEAN (no NEW findings; the lone cross-child interaction is already recorded on the 4024 pairing).

Scope note: per the latest SCOPE-DECISION comment on 6f2d, the authoritative residual scope narrowed to WS-DOC + WS-EVAL-EXISTING (WS-VISUAL-EVAL split into its own epic; WS-MIGRATE/WS-PROMPTS/WS-CLEANUP/WS-VISUAL/WS-VALIDATE/WS-INTENT-ALIGN all DONE via closed sibling epics 5766/72d5/e6d9/681b). So the 7-mode cross-child check runs WS-EVAL-EXISTING against exactly one live sibling: WS-DOC (4024).

7-mode audit (all CLEAN):
- Implicit shared state — CLEAN. Both children touch the eval seam, but the division is build (b4af) vs document (4024). b4af PRODUCES the seam (scorer registry, run_eval(), eval-discipline/eval-live CI, datasets/fixtures); 4024 READS what b4af produces ("reflects WS-EVAL-EXISTING as built"). No undeclared shared mutable artifact; b4af does not silently depend on anything 4024 mutates.
- Conflicting assumptions — CLEAN. The two-tier eval model is described identically on both sides: deterministic gate on every PR at zero API cost (b4af AC4 / 4024 AC4) + LLM-judge tier OFF the PR critical path — workflow_dispatch + weekly schedule, non-blocking, response-cached, concurrency-capped, fork-safe, spend-cap-guided (b4af AC5 / 4024 AC4). Tiers, triggers, and rails agree — no contradiction.
- Scope overlap — CLEAN. Building the seam + finishing the run machinery (b4af) vs documenting it (4024) is a clean labor split, not duplication. b4af claims no documentation deliverable; 4024 claims no build deliverable.
- Dependency gap — CLEAN (no missing producer). b4af is itself the producer the sibling depends on; b4af's own inbound dependency is the (closed/DONE) engine epic e6d9 + the migrated reviewers — not the live sibling. The un-declared sequencing of 4024-after-b4af is an ORDERING concern, captured on the 4024 pairing (see below), not a producer-absence here.
- Consumer impact — CLEAN / deferred. Per the epic's own agent-vs-single-turn bright line, G4 defers code-grounded consumer-impact to E4/G1G2/A1; no plan-level consumer breakage introduced by b4af against 4024.
- Residual references — CLEAN. b4af names no descoped/relocated workstreams as if live. The internal arithmetic reconciles against the codebase: b4af's "all 7 specs" = 4 EXISTING (code-quality.eval.yaml + plan-review-finder/verifier/isf .eval.yaml — all present under src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/) + 3 NEW (completion-verifier, ticket-quality, spec-alignment). The "run_eval() is an unwired stub / scorer names inert / eval-live placeholder" premise is grounded (src/rebar/llm/eval.py:319,347 — run_eval raises NotImplementedError when the extra is present; tests/unit/test_eval.py:262). No stale references.
- Ordering violation — NO NEW FINDING from b4af's vantage. The only cross-child ordering binding is WS-DOC's AC4 "(consistent with WS-EVAL-EXISTING)" / Scope "reflects WS-EVAL-EXISTING as built", which obligates the CONSUMER (4024) to be sequenced after b4af's eval-seam delivery. That binding lives on the 4024 side and is ALREADY RECORDED as a G4 ordering finding on the 4024 pairing (timestamp 1782579130344411001). b4af is the producer and carries no independent ordering obligation, so re-charging the same interaction here would be double-counting. Noted for completeness, not emitted as a new finding.

No NEW G4 finding emitted for this pairing: empty findings list. (The single live cross-child interaction — the un-sequenced "document-the-seam-as-built" binding — is correctly owned by, and already flagged on, the WS-DOC (4024) pairing.)

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PLAN REVIEW · PASS 1 · [T10] Infrastructure / IaC (overlay) — VERDICT: NOT-APPLICABLE (PASS, no findings).

T10 is an overlay that attaches only when the plan provisions or configures infrastructure — cloud resources, IaC (Terraform/CloudFormation/CDK/Pulumi/Ansible), or Kubernetes/Helm. This epic's deliverables do not fall in that surface:
- `docs/workflow-engine.md` — documentation only.
- Eval specs, deterministic-scorer registry, `run_eval()` — application/library code under `src/rebar/llm/`, not infra.
- `eval-discipline` / `eval-live` — GitHub Actions CI workflow definitions (CI config), not provisioned cloud resources or IaC tooling. None of T10's binary checks (remote state+locking, least-privilege IAM, drift, prevent_destroy on stateful RDS/S3/volumes, secrets manager, autoscaling/quotas, IaC teardown) have a target here.

Per ANTI-FP ("not-applicable for non-infra tickets"), the overlay does not attach.

Affirmations (the CI-layer analogues of the cost/secrets/blast-radius concerns are already handled appropriately, even though T10 proper does not apply): `eval-live` is fork-safe (no live call on fork PRs or Dependabot), concurrency-capped, response-cached, non-blocking (continue-on-error), and provider spend-cap guidance is documented (AC7); `eval-discipline` runs with no model call / zero API cost on every PR (AC6). Empty findings list for T10.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PLAN REVIEW · PASS 1 · [T11] Data-migration / backfill safety — VERDICT: NOT-APPLICABLE (PASS, empty findings).

T11 is an overlay that applies only when a plan changes a schema/persisted format or backfills persisted data. This epic's live deliverables (WS-DOC + WS-EVAL-EXISTING) do none of those:
- New documentation artifact (docs/workflow-engine.md) — a doc file, not a data store.
- New eval spec YAMLs under src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/ (completion-verifier, ticket-quality, spec-alignment) — additive new files.
- New deterministic-scorer registry of pure functions + validate_scorer — in-code, no persisted state.
- Wiring run_eval() to a live Inspect harness (replacing the NotImplementedError stub at src/rebar/llm/eval.py:319/346) — an execution path, not a format change.
- New CI workflows (eval-discipline, eval-live) — config, not data.
- New fixture/dataset corpus + "dataset schema" — these are NEW eval artifacts (gold_set/fixtures), not a migration or backfill of existing persisted production rows.

No expand-contract, batching, resumability, dual-write, or rollback obligations arise because no existing persisted format is altered and no existing data is backfilled. Per the criterion's ANTI-FP ("not-applicable for non-persisted/in-memory changes"), this PASSES with no finding.

Affirmations (T11): (a) online/expand-contract — n/a; (b) batching/scale — n/a; (c) resumability/idempotency — n/a; (d) dual-write window — n/a; (e) rollback/no-data-loss — n/a. All not-applicable; clean.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PASS 1 review (T3 Integration feasibility) — coaching note recorded; full structured findings returned to the review pass. The external integration in scope is Inspect AI (the `run_eval` live harness). It is a largely-established integration (extra pinned `inspect-ai>=0.3.221` in pyproject, guarded via `guard_import(extra="eval")`, `INSPECT_MIN_VERSION` floor in eval.py), which de-risks the dependency itself. The one asserted-but-unexercised capability is the concrete Inspect `Task` (dataset→solver→scorers→epochs) plus the "context-injection solver + stubbed/fixture-backed repo tools" path — no `from inspect_ai`/`solver`/`Task` usage exists yet (only the `NotImplementedError` seam at eval.py:319-351). See the per-criterion finding for the actionable item.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PLAN REVIEW · PASS 1 · [G6] Approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection — VERDICT: CLEAN (no findings).

Grounded the review in the live eval seam. (1) MECHANISM CORRECTNESS: the plan's premises check out against the code — `run_eval()` is a genuine NotImplementedError stub (src/rebar/llm/eval.py:319,346), `validate_scorer` exists and enforces the cross-family/temperature-0/seed/snapshot grader discipline the plan extends (eval.py:107-160), and `verify_completion`/`review_ticket`/`scan_spec` are genuinely agentic (verify_completion runs through gate_dispatch reading store+repo via tools, completion.py:169-225), which justifies the "context-injection solver + stubbed/fixture-backed repo tools" path rather than inline-feeding. The two-tier mechanism (zero-cost deterministic gate on every PR + LLM-judge tier off the critical path) achieves the regression-gating goal without putting paid model calls on the PR blocking path. (2) FITNESS: solves the named problem (a prompt edit is currently a blind cutover) rather than a proxy — Success Criterion explicitly requires an eval that SCORES output, not one that only shape-validates the spec. (3) ALTERNATIVES considered & discarded: (a) gate live LLM-judge evals directly on every PR — rejected as worse on cost/blast-radius/fork-safety, exactly what the plan's two-tier split avoids; (b) inline-feed reviewer inputs instead of a context-injection solver — not viable because the reviewers read store+repo via tools, so the chosen solver+stub approach is codebase-aligned. The chosen approach is defensibly at-least-as-good on all axes; alternatives discarded, not written into the plan. (4) POSITIVE RATIONALE present: the story states why ("regression-gated, not a blind cutover"; deterministic-cheap-PR vs expensive-judge-off-path aligned to community consensus; fork-safety/spend-cap rails).

Affirmations (G6): mechanism correctness (grounded against eval.py + completion.py); fitness-for-purpose (scores output, not shape-only); approach selection (defensible vs two structurally-different alternatives); positive rationale stated. Empty findings list for G6.

PLAN REVIEW · PASS 1 · [G6] Approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection — VERDICT: CLEAN (no findings).

Grounded against the live eval seam. (1) MECHANISM CORRECTNESS: premises verified against code — run_eval() is a genuine NotImplementedError stub (src/rebar/llm/eval.py:319,346), and verify_completion is genuinely agentic (routes through gate_dispatch.produce_completion_verdict, reading store+repo via tools, completion.py:221-225), which justifies the "context-injection solver + stubbed/fixture-backed repo tools" path rather than inline-feeding. The "all 7 specs" arithmetic reconciles: 4 existing specs present under src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/ (code-quality + plan-review-finder/verifier/isf-finder) + 3 new = 7. The two-tier mechanism (zero-cost deterministic gate on every PR + LLM-judge tier off the critical path) achieves regression-gating without paid model calls on the blocking path. (2) FITNESS: solves the named problem (prompt edit is currently a blind cutover) not a proxy — Success Criterion 2 explicitly requires an eval that SCORES output, not one that only shape-validates the spec. (3) ALTERNATIVES generated & discarded: (a) gate live LLM-judge evals directly on every PR — worse on cost/blast-radius/fork-safety, exactly what the two-tier split avoids; (b) inline-feed reviewer inputs instead of a context-injection solver — not viable since reviewers read store+repo via tools. Chosen approach defensibly at-least-as-good on all axes; alternatives discarded, not written into the plan. (4) POSITIVE RATIONALE present (story body: "regression-gated, not a blind cutover"; deterministic-cheap-PR vs expensive-judge-off-path aligned to community consensus; fork-safety/spend-cap rails). Empty findings list for G6.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (T3 — integration feasibility overlay), recorded for the author:

AFFIRM: the external dep is concretely pinned (`inspect-ai>=0.3.221` in pyproject `[eval]`), lazily guarded (`rebar._optional.guard_import(..., extra="eval")`), and auth/HTTPS preconditions are stated (provider keys via CI env: `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`/`OPENAI_API_KEY` in `.github/workflows/eval.yml`, live tier gated to credentialed dispatch). Good integration hygiene.

FINDINGS (coaching):
- [ ] AC #5 ("run_eval() runs a real Inspect eval … context-injection solver plus stubbed or fixture-backed repo tools") asserts a NEW Inspect AI capability that the codebase has never exercised (today `src/rebar/llm/eval.py:346` raises NotImplementedError; no `solver`/Task wiring exists in `src/rebar/llm`). The plan describes the integration only at capability level — it does not name or verify the specific Inspect AI primitives it depends on (`Task`, `@solver`, `@scorer`, `eval()`, `epochs`, and the tool-stubbing mechanism) against Inspect AI ≥0.3.221 docs/API (MATCH/MISMATCH/UNVERIFIED). [T3]
- [ ] The riskiest, load-bearing integration claim — injecting repo CONTEXT into the solver and STUBBING/fixture-backing the repo tools the three agentic reviewers call — has no verified Inspect AI mechanism named and no SPIKE gating it before the full build commits. If Inspect AI's solver/tool model can't host context injection + tool stubs as described, the AC is unbuildable. Consider a short SPIKE that stands up one minimal Task end-to-end (one spec, stubbed tools) to de-risk before wiring all three reviewers + CI. [T3]

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: All 4 task children closed + signed (fizzy-tenor-null registry, head-husk-stead 3 specs, sue-peer-twin solver+fixtures, tasty-suit-fiend run_eval+CI). The migrated reviewers are now regression-gated: deterministic scorer registry + strict validation; 3 disciplined eval specs; eval solver running the real agentic ops against disposable fixture stores; run_eval native live loop reusing the discipline primitives; eval-discipline gates all packaged specs offline every PR (zero cost), eval-live runs run_eval off the PR path (non-blocking, fork-safe, cost-railed). Live single-case smoke validated end-to-end. Closing.

compiled_state.description:
# WS-EVAL-EXISTING — make the migrated reviewers regression-gated

## Why

`verify_completion`, `review_ticket`, and `scan_spec` migrated onto the engine but
have NO standing eval coverage, so a prompt edit or migration is a blind cutover.
Worse, the eval LIVE tier is currently an unwired stub (`run_eval()` raises
NotImplementedError; deterministic scorer NAMES are inert strings with no registry
that executes them; the `eval-live` promptfoo step is a placeholder echo). So even the
4 existing plan-review/code-quality specs are validated for shape but never SCORED.

## What

Deliver eval coverage that actually gates, for the 3 uncovered reviewers, by also
finishing the shared eval-execution machinery (which benefits all 7 specs). Design
aligns with 2025-26 community consensus: a cheap DETERMINISTIC gate on every PR
(eval-discipline, zero-cost) and an expensive LLM-JUDGE tier OFF the PR critical path
(eval-live: manual + weekly schedule, non-blocking, cached, concurrency-capped).

## Scope (delivered via task children)

- Phase 1: author 3 eval specs (completion-verifier, ticket-quality, spec-alignment) + offline discipline tests.
- Phase 2: 2.1 deterministic-scorer registry + impls; 2.2 live solver w/ context-injection + repo-tool stubbing
  (the 3 reviewers read the store + repo via tools, so they are NOT inline-feedable); 2.3 per-reviewer dataset
  schema + validation (incl. scan_spec BATCH unit); 2.4 fixture corpus + label adjudication
  (completion-verifier FAIL findings need source-citation fixtures); 2.5 Inspect Task glue + eval-live CI wiring.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 3 packaged eval specs exist and are validate_eval_spec-clean with disciplined cross-family judges (gates:false), at_least(k)/epochs, coverage threshold, non-empty gold_set.
- [ ] A deterministic-scorer registry exists; every scorer name used by all 7 specs maps to an implemented pure function; validate_scorer rejects unregistered names.
- [ ] `run_eval()` runs live for all dataset-bearing specs (no longer NotImplementedError) via a native loop over the eval solver (Inspect AI intentionally not used — rationale in eval.py); the 3 agentic reviewers run against a disposable fixture store via the solver.
- [ ] `eval-discipline` CI gates all 7 specs on every PR at zero API cost; offline tests assert dataset balance + non-empty gold_set (validate_eval_spec does not).
- [ ] `eval-live` runs on workflow_dispatch + weekly schedule, non-blocking, response-cached, concurrency-capped, fork-safe; provider spend-cap guidance documented.
compiled_state.title: WS-EVAL-EXISTING: regression-gate completion-verifier/ticket-quality/spec-alignment (Phase 1+2)
